title: Combination Sprinkler and Standpipe FDC Operation

hoses: Yellow hydrant supply hose from hydrant to Engine 181 intake
Blue standpipe FDC supply line A: 100 ft of 2½-inch hose from Engine 181 discharge to Standpipe FDC inlet A
Blue standpipe FDC supply line B: 100 ft of 2½-inch hose from Engine 181 discharge to Standpipe FDC inlet B
Blue sprinkler FDC supply line: 100 ft of 2½-inch hose from Engine 181 discharge to Sprinkler FDC
Interior attack line A: 150 ft of 2½-inch hose from 7th-floor standpipe outlet to fire apartment
Interior attack line B: 150 ft of 2½-inch hose from 7th-floor standpipe outlet to fire apartment
nozzles: Interior attack line A with 1⅛-inch smooth bore nozzle, 265 GPM @ 50 PSI
Interior attack line B with 1⅛-inch smooth bore nozzle, 265 GPM @ 50 PSI
sceneElements.elevation: Fire apartment is on the 7th floor, using elevation pressure = height × 0.5 PSI/ft

problems.title: Total Standpipe Attack Flow
problems.question: Only the standpipe attack flows are known; the separate sprinkler FDC flowing GPM is unknown and must not be added. Two standpipe attack lines are operating at 265 GPM each. What known standpipe flow should be used for the pump operation?
formulaBreakdown: Total standpipe flow = attack line A flow + attack line B flow.
problems.instructorExplanation: The standpipe system is feeding two interior attack lines. Add both line flows for a total standpipe attack flow of 530 GPM.
problems.explainMistake: Do not count only one interior line. Both attack lines are flowing from the standpipe system.

problems.title: Flow Per Standpipe FDC Supply Line
problems.question: Engine 181 supplies the Standpipe FDC with two equal supply lines. The known standpipe demand is 530 GPM, and the separate sprinkler FDC flow is unknown and excluded. What flow should be used in each Standpipe FDC supply line?
problems.inputLabel: Enter flow per Standpipe FDC line
formulaBreakdown: Flow per Standpipe FDC line = total standpipe flow / number of Standpipe FDC supply lines.
Number of Standpipe FDC supply lines = 2.
problems.instructorExplanation: The two Standpipe FDC supply lines split the total standpipe flow equally. Each line carries 265 GPM.
problems.explainMistake: Do not calculate each Standpipe FDC supply line using the full 530 GPM. The two blue standpipe lines share the flow.

problems.title: Standpipe FDC Supply Line Friction Loss
problems.question: Each Standpipe FDC supply line is 100 ft of 2½-inch hose. The known standpipe demand is 530 GPM split equally between two supply lines. Use FL = C × (GPM/100)² × (length/100), with C = 2. What is the friction loss in each Standpipe FDC supply line?
formulaBreakdown: FL = C x (GPM / 100)^2 x hose length in hundreds.
Flow per Standpipe FDC line = 265 GPM.
problems.instructorExplanation: Each Standpipe FDC supply line carries 265 GPM. At 265 GPM through 100 ft of 2½-inch hose, friction loss rounds to 15 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not use the total 530 GPM in one Standpipe FDC supply line. Each of the two blue standpipe lines carries half the total standpipe flow.

problems.title: Standpipe Pump Discharge Pressure
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying the Standpipe FDC with two equal 100 ft 2½-inch supply lines. The known standpipe attack flow is 530 GPM; split that flow equally between the two Standpipe FDC supply lines. Each interior attack line is 150 ft of 2½-inch hose flowing 265 GPM through a 1⅛-inch smooth bore nozzle at 50 PSI. The fire is on the 7th floor. Use height = (floor number - 1) × 10 ft and elevation pressure = height × 0.5 PSI/ft. Add 25 PSI standpipe/FDC system loss. The separate Sprinkler FDC support flow is unknown and must not be added to this standpipe pump-pressure path. What pump discharge pressure should Engine 181 set for the standpipe operation?
formulaBreakdown: PP = FL + NP + elevation + appliance loss.
Friction loss = FDC supply line FL + interior attack line FL.
problems.instructorExplanation: For the standpipe operation, calculate one complete supply path from Engine 181 to the Standpipe FDC, through the standpipe system, and through the interior attack line. Add FDC supply line friction loss, interior hose friction loss, nozzle pressure, elevation, and standpipe/FDC loss for a final pump discharge pressure of 140 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not add friction loss for both equal Standpipe FDC supply lines together. Parallel lines split the flow, so one complete supply path governs the pressure.

problems.title: Sprinkler FDC Supply Hose Count
problems.question: The sprinkler FDC is separate from the Standpipe FDC. The sprinkler system flowing GPM is unknown, so it should be recorded as sprinkler support and excluded from the standpipe flow calculation. How many blue supply lines feed only the Sprinkler FDC?
problems.inputLabel: Enter number of sprinkler FDC hoses
formulaBreakdown: Sprinkler flowing GPM is unknown and is not added to the standpipe attack calculation.
Count only blue supply hoses connected to the Sprinkler FDC.
problems.instructorExplanation: The Sprinkler FDC is supplied by one blue hose from Engine 181. Its flowing GPM is unknown, so this question isolates the sprinkler support line count rather than adding sprinkler flow.
problems.explainMistake: Do not count the two Standpipe FDC hoses as sprinkler supply hoses, and do not add an assumed sprinkler GPM to the standpipe calculation.
